mission.title: Install and Configure Valkey
mission.description: Valkey is a high-performance distributed key-value database optimized for caching and real-time workloads. It is suitable for applications requiring low-latency data access and supports various data structures and plugins. Key trade-offs include the need for careful configuration and potential complexity in deployment.

steps.title: Add the Valkey Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Valkey Helm chart repository to your local Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add valkey https://github.com/valkey-io/valkey/releases/download/v9.0.3

```

> This URL points to the specific release of Valkey.

steps.title: Install Valkey CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions required for Valkey to function properly.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/valkey-io/valkey/v9.0.3/deploy/crds/valkey_crd.yaml

```

> Ensure you have the correct CRD file for the version.

steps.title: Install Valkey using Helm
steps.description:
This command installs Valkey into the specified namespace with the desired version.

```bash

helm install valkey valkey/valkey --namespace valkey --version 9.0.3

```

> Replace 'valkey' with your desired release name.

steps.title: Create a Valkey Custom Resource
steps.description:
This step creates an instance of Valkey to run in your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: valkey.io/v1
kind: Valkey
metadata:
  name: my-valkey
spec:
  replicas: 3
EOF

```

> This creates a Valkey instance with 3 replicas.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: valkey.io/v1
kind: Valkey
metadata:
  name: my-valkey
spec:
  replicas: 3
EOF

steps.title: Verify Valkey is running
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the Valkey pods to ensure they are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n valkey

```

> Look for the status 'Running' for all Valkey pods.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Valkey
uninstall.description:
This step removes Valkey from your cluster. Be aware that this will delete all associated data and resources.

```bash

helm uninstall valkey --namespace valkey

kubectl delete namespace valkey

```

> This command will remove the Valkey release.
> This will delete the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Valkey
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you backup your data. This step will upgrade Valkey to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl cp my-valkey-0:/data /backup/my-valkey-backup

helm upgrade valkey valkey/valkey --namespace valkey --version 9.0.3

kubectl get pods -n valkey

```

> Backup your data from the Valkey pod.
> This command upgrades Valkey to the specified version.
> Verify that the upgraded pods are running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to Valkey'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the Valkey service is not running or the network policy is blocking access.

**Fix:**
Check the status of the Valkey pods and ensure network policies allow traffic.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Valkey pod is CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Valkey pod is failing to start, possibly due to misconfiguration.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n valkey' for errors.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This error occurs when the cluster does not have enough resources to schedule the Valkey pods.

**Fix:**
Increase the resource limits or add more nodes to the cluster.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Custom Resource not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Valkey Custom Resource Definition (CRD) is not installed or is outdated.

**Fix:**
Reapply the CRD using 'kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/valkey-io/valkey/v9.0.3/deploy/crds/valkey_crd.yaml'.

versionNotes.changes: Introduced support for enhanced data structures and improved performance for caching workloads.
versionNotes.deprecations: No specific deprecations noted in this release.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from version 8.x, ensure to review the new configuration options in the documentation.

resolution.summary: You should see the Valkey pods listed with 'Running' status when executing 'kubectl get pods -n valkey'.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: valkey.io/v1
kind: Valkey
metadata:
  name: my-valkey
spec:
  replicas: 3
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the necessary tools installed.
